---
title: "Running pipelines"
description: "Launch any OpenPipeline workflow locally, on Seqera, or through Viash Hub."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 80
---

Every guide's `nextflow run` command works the same way; this page covers the
**mechanics of launching** on each of the three routes. For a full first run with
real data, see [Get started](../get-started/index.qmd); for CPU / memory tuning,
see [Tune compute resources](tune-resources.qmd).

Whichever route you pick, workflows are pulled from **Viash Hub** and every step
runs inside a container, so you never install a pipeline's dependencies yourself.

## Local (Nextflow)

Run on your own machine, an HPC node, or a cloud VM. You need Nextflow and Docker
(see [Get started](../get-started/index.qmd) for the install), and a one-time
registration of the Viash Hub source:

```bash
mkdir -p "$HOME/.nextflow"
cat >> "$HOME/.nextflow/scm" <<'EOM'
providers.vsh.platform = "gitea"
providers.vsh.server = "packages.viash-hub.com"
EOM
```

Then launch any workflow by pointing `-main-script` at it and passing your
parameters:

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/process_samples/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml --publish_dir results/
```

::: {.callout-warning}
OpenPipeline's workflows don't yet run under the strict language syntax enforced
by the newest Nextflow releases. Pin a compatible version before running:
`export NXF_VER=25.10.2`.
:::

## Seqera Platform

No local install. Set up a [compute environment](https://docs.seqera.io/platform/latest/compute-envs/overview)
(AWS / Azure / GCP / Kubernetes / HPC) once, then launch with the `tw` CLI:

```bash
tw launch https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline \
  --revision 4.2.0 --pull-latest \
  --main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/process_samples/main.nf \
  --compute-env <your-env-id> --params-file params.yaml
```

Or launch and monitor the same workflow from the Seqera **Launchpad** web UI, no
CLI required.

## Viash Hub

No install, no CLI. Open the package in the [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com)
catalog, pick a workflow, and fill in the guided web form (every parameter is
rendered with its description). Choose local execution or a connected Seqera
environment and press **Launch**.

:::{.callout-note}
The `-main-script` / `--main-script` path is the only thing that changes between
workflows, swap in any workflow from the [reference](../reference/index.qmd). Input
and parameters go in a [`param_list`](process-many-samples.qmd) / params file.
:::
